questions.question:
Which one(s) of the following structures cannot represent resonance forms for N2O (diamagnetic)?
 $:\ddot N = N = \ddot O:$
 $:N \equiv N - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} :$
 $:\underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot N} - N \equiv \mathop O\limits_.^. :$
 $:\ddot N = O = \ddot N:$
 $:\mathop N\limits_.^. = N = \mathop O\limits_.^. :$
questions.question_raw:
Which one(s) of the following structures cannot represent resonance forms for N2O (diamagnetic)?

  1. {tex}:\ddot N = N = \ddot O:{/tex}
  2. {tex}:N \equiv N - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} :{/tex}
  3. {tex}:\underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot N} - N \equiv \mathop O\limits_.^. :{/tex}
  4. {tex}:\ddot N = O = \ddot N:{/tex}
  5. {tex}:\mathop N\limits_.^. = N = \mathop O\limits_.^. :{/tex}

questions.question:
Statement-1: In trigonal bipyramidal geometry the multiple bonds get positioned preferably in the equatorial plane.
Statement-2: Equatorial position minimise the overall repulsion of $\pi$-bond because $\pi$-bond require more space compared to the $\sigma$-bond pair.
Statement-3: The equatorial bonds are relatively shorter, this reduced bond length favour the better $\pi$-interaction in the equatorial plane.

Statement-3: The equatorial bonds are relatively shorter, this reduced bond length favour the better {tex}\pi{/tex}-interaction in the equatorial plane.

options.text: Statement-1, Statement-2, Statement-3 are correct and Statement-1 is explained by Statement-2 but not by Statement-3
Statement-1, Statement-2, Statement-3 all are correct and Statement-2, Statement-3 both are correct explanation of Statement-1
Statement-1 and Statement-3 are correct, Statement-2 is incorrect, and Statement-3 is the correct explanation of Statement-1
Statement-1, Statement-2 are correct, Statement-3 is incorrect and statement-2 is the correct explanation of statement-1

Match List-I with List-II.
 List - I
 List - II
 (A) $\Psi_{\mathrm{MO}}=\Psi_{\mathrm{A}}-\Psi_{\mathrm{B}}$
 (I) Dipole moment
 (B) $\mu=\mathbf{Q} \times \mathbf{r}$
 (II) Bonding molecular orbital
 (C) $\frac{N_{\mathrm{b}}-N_{\mathrm{a}}}{2}$
 (III) Anti-bonding molecular orbital
 (D) $\Psi_{\mathrm{MO}}=\Psi_{\mathrm{A}}+\Psi_{\mathrm{B}}$
 (IV) Bond order
questions.question_raw:
Match List-I with List-II.

List - I List - II
(A) {tex}\Psi_{\mathrm{MO}}=\Psi_{\mathrm{A}}-\Psi_{\mathrm{B}}{/tex} (I) Dipole moment
(B) {tex}\mu=\mathbf{Q} \times \mathbf{r}{/tex} (II) Bonding molecular orbital
(C) {tex}\frac{N_{\mathrm{b}}-N_{\mathrm{a}}}{2}{/tex} (III) Anti-bonding molecular orbital
(D) {tex}\Psi_{\mathrm{MO}}=\Psi_{\mathrm{A}}+\Psi_{\mathrm{B}}{/tex} (IV) Bond order
